title: uragoga lagoensis
side_effects: Uragoga lagoensis can have side effects if used improperly, such as nausea, vomiting, and stomach cramps due to its high concentration of saponins and glycosides. Prolonged use can also lead to kidney damage and electrolyte imbalance, which may cause muscle weakness, fatigue, and dizziness. Additionally, improper dosage can cause allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and respiratory problems. It is essential to consult with a medical professional before using uragoga lagoensis for any health benefits to minimize the risk of adverse effects.
